article_title: European Commission Confirms Cyberattack on AWS Cloud Account as Hacker Claims 350 GB of Stolen Data

content_preview.title: European Commission Confirms Cyberattack on AWS Cloud Account as Hacker Claims 350 GB of Stolen Data
content_preview.summary: The EU's executive arm is investigating a breach of its Amazon Web Services account that exposed Europa.eu infrastructure, the second cloud incident to hit the institution in 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Commission has confirmed a cyberattack targeting its Amazon Web Services cloud account, with a threat actor claiming to have exfiltrated more than 350 gigabytes of data including employee information and databases. The breach, detected on March 24, affected the cloud infrastructure hosting the Commission's public web presence on the Europa.eu platform, according to [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/european-commission-investigating-breach...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.source_verification: TechCrunch confirmed EC cyberattack, 350 GB claim, March 24, employee data.
